title: Aging workstream marks canonical code while module remains NOT_CANONICAL
description: The Aging workstream row reports canonical_code_status=YES even though the authoritative module-status table still marks Aging as NOT_CANONICAL with CANONICAL_PIPELINE=NO. This creates contradictory operational signaling about Aging canonical readiness.

title: Relaxation workstream marks canonical code while module remains NOT_CANONICAL
description: The Relaxation workstream row reports canonical_code_status=YES even though the authoritative module-status table still marks Relaxation as NOT_CANONICAL with CANONICAL_PIPELINE=NO. This creates contradictory operational signaling about Relaxation canonical readiness.

title: Relaxation saved-output scripts treat a NOT_CANONICAL module as canonical evidence
description: Several analysis/report builders describe Relaxation saved tables as canonical inputs or outputs even though the module-status table still says Relaxation is NOT_CANONICAL and cross-module participation remains blocked until all participating modules are canonical. This wording can make advisory saved outputs look like canonical closure.

title: Aging structural collapse is reused as canonical cross-module evidence before canonicalization
description: The unified crossover synthesis uses an Aging structural-collapse sweep as canonical evidence and escalates to paper-ready messaging, while a separate Aging artifact explicitly warns the pipeline is before full canonicalization. That mix risks treating saved structural outputs as canonical closure evidence instead of advisory WIP material.

title: MT ver2 is active but absent from canonicalization coverage tables
description: The system registry marks MT ver2 active and the repository already carries explicit MT canonicalization blockers, but MT ver2 has no row in module_canonical_status and no workstream coverage row. This leaves an active module with known canonicalization debt outside the progress-tracking layer.
